title: Doha 2-Day Luxury Escape
metaTitle: Doha 2-Day Luxury Escape | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Doha, Qatar: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Museum of Islamic Art and National Museum of Qatar. Prices from $1397 per person (estimate).
highlights: Desert dune bashing and sandboarding at Sealine
Sealine desert and Inland Sea (Khor Al Adaid)
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Doha, Qatar — Desert climate — extremely hot, humid summers (highs 40-42°C) and mild, dry winters (highs around 23-25°C). November to March, for warm but comfortable temperatures and lower humidity than summer. This itinerary blends Museum of Islamic Art, National Museum of Qatar, Souq Waqif with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Museum of Islamic Art, National Museum of Qatar, Souq Waqif
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Doha
itinerary.description: Land at Hamad International Airport (DOH) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Museum of Islamic Art — I. M. Pei-designed museum of Islamic art spanning 1,400 years. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Doha Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to National Museum of Qatar — Jean Nouvel-designed museum shaped like a desert rose crystal. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to DOH for your onward flight.

travelTips: Both major museums (Islamic Art and National Museum) are free — budget a half-day for each.
Visit Souq Waqif in the evening when it's liveliest and coolest.
Book a desert safari through a licensed operator for both safety and quality.
Public transport (Metro) is excellent for West Bay and Msheireb but taxis are needed for most other areas.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Modest clothing covering shoulders and knees for public areas and Souq Waqif
Lightweight breathable fabrics for the heat
A light jacket for strongly air-conditioned malls and museums
thingsToDo: Desert safari and dune bashing, Sealine
Katara Cultural Village amphitheater and galleries
Museum of Islamic Art and National Museum of Qatar
Desert dune bashing and sandboarding at Sealine
Dhow boat sailing on the bay
thingsToAvoid: Wearing revealing clothing outside hotel pool/beach areas.
Scheduling outdoor sightseeing during summer midday heat.
Assuming alcohol is available outside licensed hotel venues.
topRestaurants: IDAM by Alain Ducasse — French fine dining ($$$$)
instagramSpots: Museum of Islamic Art at sunset
adventureActivities: Desert dune bashing and sandboarding at Sealine
Dhow boat sailing on the bay
luxuryExperiences: Fine dining at IDAM by Alain Ducasse
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset dhow cruise along the Corniche
Private desert camp dinner under the stars
Couples spa day at a Pearl-Qatar resort
budgetExperiences: Free entry to the Museum of Islamic Art and National Museum of Qatar
localEtiquette: Dress modestly in public — shoulders and knees covered, especially at Souq Waqif and government buildings.
Public displays of affection are discouraged.
Friday is the start of the weekend; some venues open later or close for Friday prayers.
Alcohol is served only in licensed hotel bars and restaurants.
localTransportGuide: Doha Metro is modern, fast, and covers key areas including West Bay and Msheireb; taxis and rideshare apps (Karwa, Uber) cover everywhere else easily and affordably. Doha is not very walkable between neighborhoods due to heat and highway-scale roads.
bestTimeToVisit: November to March, for warm but comfortable temperatures and lower humidity than summer.
weather: Desert climate — extremely hot, humid summers (highs 40-42°C) and mild, dry winters (highs around 23-25°C).
visaInformation: Visa-free entry on arrival for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India, typically for stays up to 30 days.
averageFlightTime: ~9.3h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Doha?
faqs.answer: Visa-free entry on arrival for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India, typically for stays up to 30 days.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Doha?
faqs.answer: November to March, for warm but comfortable temperatures and lower humidity than summer. Desert climate — extremely hot, humid summers (highs 40-42°C) and mild, dry winters (highs around 23-25°C).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Doha safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Doha is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Doha?
faqs.answer: The local currency is QAR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: It also works well for families — highlights include Aspire Park.

reviews.author: James Rossi
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Our trip to Doha was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to The Pearl-Qatar boat cruise was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Emma Rossi
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: Our trip to Doha was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Museum of Islamic Art and National Museum of Qatar was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Daniel Andersen
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Katara Cultural Village amphitheater and galleries and Corniche promenade. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Ava Andersen
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: National Museum of Qatar alone was worth the trip, but combined with Corniche promenade this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Ethan Kowalski
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Souq Waqif and Katara Beach. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Tom Santos
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Desert safari and dune bashing, Sealine and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
